reasoning: With less than two months until the resolution date (July 1, 2025), the probability of Beyond Meat filing for bankruptcy is extremely low. On May 7, 2025, the company announced its Q1 earnings, reporting a cash balance of $115.8 million against an operating cash burn of $26.1 million for the quarter. Crucially, they simultaneously announced a fresh $100 million secured financing facility from an affiliate of the Ahimsa Foundation. Coupled with the fact that their major $1.1 billion convertible note debt does not mature until March 2027 and the company confirming it has 'no near-term debt maturities', there is no liquidity crisis or debt deadline that would force a Chapter 11 filing in the next 54 days. Analysts and industry watchers largely cite 2027 as the base case for a potential bankruptcy filing if the balance sheet isn't restructured before then. I'm predicting 5%, the lowest allowed by the system constraints.
